channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I catch AI code mistakes before they break my project
item.description: Hi Dev community, I built a VS Code AI copilot that not only generates code but also catches subtle AI mistakes before they reach your repo. It forces structured specs, lets you refine requests,...

item.title: Why Selenium Gets Detected? How to Hide the Fact of Browser Automation
item.description: Every automation engineer eventually hits "The Wall." You write a script. It works flawlessly on your local machine. It passes staging. Then, you point it at production or a sophisticated target...

item.title: How the new iPhone 17E stacks up against Apple’s pricier phones
item.description: The iPhone 17 lineup now has a “budget” option. Announced this week, the iPhone 17E will hit stores on Wednesday, March 11th, starting at $599, with preorders opening on March 4th. The 6.1-inch...

item.title: Tsonnet #31 - Taking back control of equality
item.description: Welcome to the Tsonnet series! If you're not following along, check out how it all started in the first post of the series. In the previous post, I implemented the equality operator: Tsonnet #30 -...

item.title: From Vibe Coding to Spec-Driven Development (Building a Car Classifieds App)
item.description: AI coding tools make it incredibly easy to build quick prototypes. Most developers are familiar with “vibe coding” — prompting an AI in chat and getting a working application almost instantly. For...

item.title: Solved: how I used Webflow + automation + SEO to replace $1.2K/month in Upwork leads
item.description: 🚀 Executive Summary TL;DR: Freelancers relying on platforms like Upwork face dependency and inconsistent lead flow, leading to commoditization. The solution involves building an owned, automated...

item.title: Meet BlokJS - 9 KB, No Build Step, Standalone, Full FE Framework
item.description: BlokJS - Zero-Build, Zero-Dependency, Standalone, Reactive, Lightweight UI Framework New project, new frontend. Install Node. Pick a bundler. Configure TypeScript. Install a router package....

item.title: Expanding the Dataset: A Comprehensive Guide to SQL Joins and Window Functions
item.description: Joins and Window Functions in SQL Joins and Window Functions in SQL are two concepts that significantly elevate querying skills. These tools allow you to combine datasets and perform advanced...

item.title: GoodMonkey - 57% Reduction* in Claude Code Context via Extensible Proxy
item.description: I've been running Claude Code since it launched — long sessions, heavy tool use, complex multi-file work. And I kept hitting the same wall: around 200 turns, the model starts losing track....

item.title: Apple might use Google servers to store data for its upgraded AI Siri
item.description: Apple has asked Google to look into "setting up servers" for a new version of Siri that's Gemini-powered and meets Apple's privacy requirements, The Information reports. Apple had already...

item.title: Design a Rate Limiter
item.description: Rate Limiting — System Design Deep Dive A rate limiter is a piece of software that regulates how much traffic a client can send to a server within a given period of time. At scale, rate limiting...

item.title: I Killed MarketingNow and Built XPilot Instead. Here's Why.
item.description: I spent months building MarketingNow, watched it flatline in real time, and then built the right product in a fraction of the time. This is the story of what went wrong, what I actually learned...

item.title: Mastodon now has a button for sharing content from other websites
item.description: Sharing content to Mastodon is getting easier with the addition of a universal "Share to Mastodon" button for the decentralized social media platform. Announced on Monday, the widget allows users...

item.title: You could be an influencer without even realizing it
item.description: In late February, Puck reported on a strange case: An influencer with more than a million followers was inadvertently promoting products on Instagram. On some of Julia Berolzheimer's posts, a...

item.title: Users are ditching ChatGPT for Claude. Here’s how to make the switch
item.description: Following controversies surrounding ChatGPT, many users are ditching the AI chatbot for Claude instead. Here's how to make the switch.

item.title: Donut Lab’s solid-state battery can handle the (extreme) heat, test says
item.description: Finnish startup Donut Lab is out to prove that its solid-state battery is all that it claims to be. Last week, the test was about fast charging. This week is where things get hot. Like the first...

item.title: AI-generated art can’t be copyrighted after Supreme Court declines to review the rule
item.description: The US Supreme Court has declined to hear a case over whether AI-generated art can obtain a copyright, as reported earlier by Reuters. The Monday decision comes after Stephen Thaler, a computer...

item.title: Here’s the upgrade to my favorite phone camera of last year
item.description: Vivo rarely has a presence at MWC, but this year it bucked that trend in a big way, with the reveal of its next flagship phone, the X300 Ultra, alongside an upgraded telephoto extender lens and...

item.title: OpenAI’s “compromise” with the Pentagon is what Anthropic feared
item.description: On February 28, OpenAI announced it had reached a deal that will allow the US military to use its technologies in classified settings. CEO Sam Altman said the negotiations, which the company began...

item.title: Tech workers urge DOD, Congress to withdraw Anthropic label as a supply-chain risk
item.description: Tech workers have signed an open letter urging the Department of Defense to withdraw its designation of Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" and instead to settle the matter quietly.

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the...

item.title: Nvidia’s spending $4 billion on photonics to stay ahead of the curve in AI
item.description: Nvidia announced on Monday that it's investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, which are both developing photonics technology for data centers, like optical transceivers, circuit...

item.title: Google Develops Merkle Tree Certificates to Enable Quantum-Resistant HTTPS in Chrome
item.description: Google has announced a new program in its Chrome browser to ensure that HTTPS certificates are secure against the future risk posed by quantum computers. "To ensure the scalability and efficiency...

item.title: The Analogue Pocket will be back in stock and slightly more expensive
item.description: Analogue announced a restock for its Pocket handheld, but for the second time since late 2021, it's getting a price bump, this time to $239.99. The Pocket will once again be available for preorder...

item.title: Magic Keyboard cases for the latest iPad Pro are up to $85 off
item.description: Apple’s Magic Keyboard cases offer a fantastic typing experience, elevating the iPad to laptop status with a large trackpad that offers haptic feedback, and an articulating hinge to help you angle...

item.title: A married founder duo’s company, 14.ai, is replacing customer support teams at startups
item.description: 14.ai also launched a consumer brand to understand how much AI can handle customer support tasks.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
item.description: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access...

item.title: The Download: protesting AI, and what’s floating in space
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests ever...

item.title: I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests ever
item.description: Pull the plug! Pull the plug! Stop the slop! Stop the slop! For a few hours this Saturday, February 28, I watched as a couple of hundred anti-AI protesters marched through London’s King’s Cross...
